Nick Morgan's Visit, Class Reflections, and Navigating Tensions

04-20-2026

Nick Morgan's visit left me thinking about how security isn't a layer you bolt on at the end. It's a mindset woven into every decision you make while building. What stood out most was how he framed vulnerability not as a failure of intelligence but as a failure of attention. One of the most intruiging points that Nick made was when he discussed Red Team vs Blue Team; the difference between thinking and acting as an attacker versus a defender of a system.


Reflecting on the Course and Its Tensions

As a student in this class, I was able to identify benefits and downsides, on weekly basis. A learning goal where this class exceled was improving the student's ability to make decisions about open-ended project, especially when AI is involved. Each week, we were encouraged to consult with our partner BEFORE consulting AI on our project. This gave us a chance to create a list of tasks or a framework for what each aspect of our project will look like. This way, we can get most of the details down and use AI for more technical work or cloudy details.

While I enjoyed the decision-making aspect of this class, it failed to challenge me with regards to coordinating needs for "clients". The needs for our Social Network are relatively straight forward, requiring moderately intense code review. An idea for future sessions of this course; each group has to choose one aspect of the project where they will expand extensively using their AI agent. This would allow each group to consider challenging client needs beyond the basic implementation of the project.

To me, the mose useful part of this course was the blogging portion. As stated in the course's learning objectives, "humans excel at making tools, but they also need to make words". AI can point a human to almost tool that you could ask for, but what it will always struggle to do is connect with a human. Writing blogs this semester has helped me turn technical thoughts into concrete discussions that can spark human connection. I'm grateful to have improved this skill before I go out into the crazy work and job market that we're living in. Written and verbal communication will always be a valuable skill, no matter what the job market looks like.


Nogramming Assignment

I have part of my LLM built, I will start putting large pieces together to begin finalizing it.

); } export default Twelve;
